checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=370340000 (FY2009) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=620255000 (FY2012), ~67% growth over same span

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=90191000 (2009-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=126335000 (2012-12-31), a 40% rise vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of ~67% over the same span

GrossProfit=309230000 (FY2009) / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=370340000 (FY2009) = 83.5% margin; GrossProfit=466846000 (FY2012) / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=620255000 (FY2012) = 75.3% margin - a gradual, plausible margin decline rather than an inconsistency suggesting manipulation

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=61932000 (Q4 2010) vs NetIncomeLoss=1088000 (Q1 2010); NetIncomeLoss=-67552000 (Q3 2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=5380000 (Q2 2011)
NetIncomeLoss=-69849000 (FY2011) followed by NetIncomeLoss=3144000 (FY2012) - large swings rather than smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=4163000 (2009-12-31), ratio to AR ~7.1%, declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2780000 (2012-12-31), ratio to AR ~3.1%, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 58462000 to 88958000 over the same span
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=25989000 (2010-12-31) declining to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=20880000 (2012-12-31) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 478237000 (FY2010) to 620255000 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2010-05-26, shortly after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26
Elevated 8-K frequency in late 2011/2012 (e.g., 8-Ks filed 2011-09-21, 2011-09-27, 2011-09-28, 2011-09-29, 2011-10-07, 2011-10-27) coinciding with the Q3 2011 net loss period

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, GrossProfit, SalesRevenueGoodsNet spanning FY2008 through Q1 2013

top_signals: Persistent and growing divergence between net income and operating cash flow, most pronounced in FY2011 (NetIncomeLoss=-69849000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=62965000) and FY2012 (NetIncomeLoss=3144000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=130082000), plausibly explained by a large litigation accrual and tax items but still notable in scale.

Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of receivables declined from ~7.1% (2009) to ~3.1% (2012) while receivables grew, and accrued current liabilities fell in absolute terms (AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=25989000 in 2010 to 20880000 in 2012) despite ~30% revenue growth over the same period - a pattern consistent with reserve/liability under-accrual though also consistent with improved collections.

Other non-current assets grew far faster than revenue (OtherAssetsNoncurrent=7260000 in 2009 to 25463000 in 2012, +251% vs ~67% revenue growth) and a large one-time Goodwill step-up in 2011 (103070000 to 159349000), warranting scrutiny of asset capitalization/acquisition accounting though dollar amounts are modest relative to total assets (~$1.16B).
